chapter_title.en: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions

text: 3621 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي يَعْفُورٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ : "مَا مِنْ نَفَقَةٍ أَحَبَّ إِلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ نَفَقَةِ قَصْدٍ وَ يُبْغِضُ اَلْإِسْرَافَ إِلاَّ فِي اَلْحَجِّ وَ اَلْعُمْرَةِ فَرَحِمَ اَللَّهُ مُؤْمِناً كَسَبَ طَيِّباً وَ أَنْفَقَ مِنْ قَصْدٍ أَوْ قَدَّمَ فَضْلاً ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3621 - Ibn Abi Ya'fur narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
'There is no spending more beloved to Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, than moderate spending. He (swt) dislikes extravagance, except in Hajj and Umrah.
May Allah (swt) have mercy on a believer who earns lawfully, spends moderately, or gives something in charity from his surplus.' "
